Run Toy Data Through the Models

This notebook runs on **synthetic (mock) toy data**, not real patient records.

Load Libraries
```{r}
if (!requireNamespace("tidyverse", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("tidyverse")
}
library(tidyverse)
```

Load Toy Data
```{r}
# Synthetic dataset — fabricated records used only to exercise the pipeline.
toy_data_breast_ovary <- read.csv("toy_data_breast_ovary.csv")

```

Load Pre-Defined Model
```{r}
# Saved  LASSO coefficients from the penalized Fine-Gray model seen in Supplementary Table 5
coef_df <- readRDS("final_model_breast_ovary_se_coefs.rds")

```



Run Model on Toy Data
```{r}
# Check that all model predictors are present in the toy dataset.
stopifnot(all(coef_df$variable %in% names(toy_data_breast_ovary)))


# Build the design matrix using ONLY the model's predictors, in coefficient order.
X_toy_data <- as.matrix(toy_data_breast_ovary[, coef_df$variable, drop = FALSE])

# Linear predictor (X %*% beta).
# NOTE: this is a relative risk score for ranking patients.
lp_mpc_predict <- as.numeric(X_toy_data %*% coef_df$coefficient)
```

Plot Density of Linear Predictor
```{r}
# The x-axis is the relative risk score; use it to compare/rank patients,
# top 5% highest-risk patients within this cohort.
d_ranking <- density(lp_mpc_predict)
cutoff_95_ranking <- quantile(lp_mpc_predict, 0.95)
plot(d_ranking,
     main = "Density Plot of Linear Predictor (Risk Score)",
     xlab = "Linear Predictor (Risk Score)",
     col  = "steelblue",
     lwd  = 2)
polygon(d_ranking, col = "lightblue", border = "steelblue")
# Top 5% cutoff (relative, within-cohort)
abline(v = cutoff_95_ranking, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
text(x = cutoff_95_ranking,
     y = max(d_ranking$y) * 0.9,
     labels = "High Risk (top 5%)",
     pos = 4,
     col = "red")
```
